sources.exact_document_title: Clinical Management Guideline: Keratitis, contact-lens-associated infiltrative
sources.population: Contact-lens wearers with suspected contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis features and safe lens-care documentation; UAE escalation pathways require clinician review.

exact_sections.heading: Clinical summary — presentation and differential safety
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.

exact_sections.heading: Patient summary — lens cessation, hygiene, and wearing schedules
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented temporary lens cessation, hand and lens-case hygiene, avoidance of overnight wear, wearing-schedule review, and reassessment context.

archive.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Keratitis, contact-lens-associated infiltrative
archive.population: Contact-lens wearers with suspected contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Clinical summary — presentation and differential safety

sources.issuing_organisation: Tear Film & Ocular Surface Society Dry Eye Workshop II Diagnostic Methodology Subcommittee
sources.exact_document_title: TFOS DEWS II Diagnostic Methodology report
sources.population: People undergoing dry-eye symptom screening, diagnosis, subtype assessment, differential diagnosis, or monitoring.
sources.clinical_setting: Ocular-surface symptom assessment and eye-care diagnostic evaluation.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected dry-eye symptom, diagnostic-test, ocular-surface, eyelid, and contact-lens context. It does not support primary-care autonomous diagnosis, treatment, medicine, referral urgency, fixed follow-up, or UAE service pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Clinical protocol for the dry-eye diagnostic test battery
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports dry-eye symptom screening, visual symptoms, tear-film tests, fluorescein or lissamine staining, and cornea, conjunctiva, or eyelid-margin observation without inferring diagnosis from symptoms alone.

exact_sections.heading: Ocular-surface damage and eyelid assessment
exact_sections.locator: Sections 6.6 and 6.8; official report lines 327–365 and 402–463
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-observed corneal, conjunctival, eyelid, and fluorescein-staining documentation in dry-eye evaluation.

exact_sections.heading: Differential diagnosis, visual asthenopia, and contact lenses
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports screen-related visual discomfort, pain or photophobia as differential context, and contact-lens-associated dry-eye context without treatment instructions.
